Director/PDMR Shareholding
Mercantile Ports & Logistics Limited (AIM: MPL), which is operating and continuing to develop a port and logistics facility in Navi Mumbai, Maharashtra, India, announces that that on 9 March 2026, Jeremy Warner Allen, Non-Executive Chairman of the Company, has transferred, via sale and purchase (the "Transfer"), Ordinary Shares into his ISA. Mr Allen sold 3,827,494 Ordinary Shares at a price of 0.313 pence and purchased 3,827,494 Ordinary Shares at a price of 0.314 pence to be held in his ISA.
Following the transfer, Mr Allen's beneficial shareholding remains unchanged at 3,827,994 Ordinary Shares, representing
1.07
% of the Company's issued share capital.

latest.title: Update on Ongoing Legal Proceedings
latest.content:
18 Dec 2025 15:29
RNS Number : 1776M
Mercantile Ports & Logistics Ltd
18 December 2025
Mercantile Ports & Logistics Limited
(
"MPL"
or the
"Company"
)
Update
Mercantile
Ports & Logistics Limited (AIM: MPL), which is operating and continuing to develop a port and logistics facility in Navi Mumbai, Maharashtra, India, provides
an update on the ongoing legal proceedings relating to its subsidiary, Karanja Terminal & Logistics Pvt. Ltd.
A civil appeal was listed before the Supreme Court of India earlier today, in which the appellant sought to set aside the interim order previously granted in MPL's favour by the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT) on 10 December 2025.
Following submissions from all parties, the Supreme Court did not pass any adverse order against the Company and directed the NCLAT to hear the matter on 14 January 2026, and, if possible, to dispose of the appeal on that date.
Importantly, the interim order dated 10 December 2025 remains in force.
The Company will continue to pursue all legal remedies available to protect its interests and will provide further updates as appropriate.
For further information, please visit
www.mercpl.com
or contact:
MPL
c/o Cavendish
+44 (0) 207 220 0500
Cavendish Capital Markets Limited
(Nomad and Broker)
Stephen Keys
+44 (0) 207 220 0500

latest.title: Update
latest.content:
17 Dec 2025 09:37
RNS Number : 9350L
Mercantile Ports & Logistics Ltd
17 December 2025
Mercantile Ports & Logistics Limited
(
"MPL"
or the
"Company"
)
Update
Mercantile
Ports & Logistics Limited (AIM: MPL), which is operating and continuing to develop a port and logistics facility in Navi Mumbai, Maharashtra, India, provides an update on the legal position and
confirms that it continues to pursue vigorously all legal remedies to safeguard its wholly owned strategic asset, Karanja Terminal & Logistics Pvt. Ltd. ("KTPL"), located in Navi Mumbai, India.
The Company recently secured interim protection from the Insolvency Appellate Tribunal, which restrains the solicitation of expressions of interest from third parties, thereby preserving the status quo while legal proceedings are ongoing.
MPL has also formally offered to redeem the full outstanding debt relating to the asset, exercising its lawful right to settlement. The debt had previously been assigned to Prudent ARC, a transaction which MPL is contesting and which forms part of ongoing legal proceedings.
Prudent ARC has since appealed against the interim order to the Supreme Court of India. The matter is scheduled to be heard on Thursday, 18 December 2025.
MPL remains confident in the strength of its legal position and is committed to defending shareholder value while ensuring that due process is followed.
The Company continues to enjoy the support of its major global stakeholders.
Commenting on the development, Pav Bakhshi, Managing Director of Mercantile Ports & Logistics, said:
"We have made a credible and lawful offer to redeem the outstanding debt in full. Karanja is a strategic infrastructure asset that rightfully belongs to MPL, and we will continue to pursue all legal avenues to protect the Company's interests and those of its shareholders."
Further announcements will be made as developments unfold.
For further information, please visit
www.mercpl.com
or contact:
MPL
c/o Cavendish
+44 (0) 207 220 0500
Cavendish Capital Markets Limited
(Nomad and Broker)
Stephen Keys
+44 (0) 207 220 0500

latest.title: Result of General Meeting
latest.content:
15 Dec 2025 11:17
RNS Number : 6006L
Mercantile Ports & Logistics Ltd
15 December 2025
Mercantile Ports & Logistics Limited
(
"MPL"
or the
"Company"
)
Result of General Meeting
Mercantile
Ports & Logistics Limited (AIM: MPL), which is operating and continuing to develop a port and logistics facility in Navi Mumbai, Maharashtra, India, is pleased to announce that further to the Company's announcement of 19 November 2025, at the General Meeting held earlier today the Resolutions proposed were duly passed by shareholders.
Application will be made to the London Stock Exchange for the cancellation of the Redemption Shares and admission of the Subscription Shares to trading on AIM. It is expected that admission of the Subscription Shares will occur and that dealings will commence at 8.00 a.m. on 16 December 2025.
Unless otherwise defined herein, capitalised terms in this announcement have the same meaning as in the shareholder circular published on 19 November 2025.
For further information, please visit
www.mercpl.com
or contact:
MPL
c/o Cavendish
+44 (0) 207 220 0500

(Nomad and Broker)

company_30s.what_they_do: Mercantile Ports & Logistics Limited operates container terminals and freight handling facilities, providing storage, loading, and customs clearance services for shipping lines and cargo owners.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Ports are the backbone of global trade; the company supplies critical infrastructure that keeps goods moving efficiently across the UK and into Europe, making it a key player in the logistics chain.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is currently in a survival mode, juggling court‑ordered restrictions and a tight liquidity profile while trying to secure new contracts and refinance debt. It is trading at a very low price that reflects its recent financial distress and legal uncertainty.

rns_narrative: The company has been fighting a series of legal challenges, including a Delhi High Court interim order that still lingers. In December 2025 it reported that it had secured a loan facility to shore up cash, but the terms are tight and the debt‑to‑equity ratio remains high.

Corporate governance updates show a reshuffle of director shareholdings and a notice of a related‑party transaction at the latest general meeting, signalling a push to align management incentives with shareholder interests.

Financially, the latest interim results painted a picture of continued losses but a slight improvement in operational throughput, hinting that the business may be stabilising if it can win new terminal contracts.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few traders believe the court ruling could unlock significant asset value and that the new loan facility will give the company breathing room to win new contracts.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Many are worried the ongoing legal battles and weak cash flow will push the company into insolvency, especially if the loan terms are too restrictive or if the court denies relief.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has moved from neutral speculation in early 2025 to a more pessimistic stance in early 2026 as legal deadlines passed without favourable outcomes.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, giving the market a chance for a surprise move if any catalyst lands.

near_term_catalysts: A potential settlement of the Delhi High Court case could lift regulatory restrictions and open up new revenue streams.
Completion of the loan facility and the announcement of a new terminal contract would provide the cash cushion needed for growth.
Any positive commentary from major shipping lines about the company’s service quality could spark renewed investor interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike that exceeds 150% of the 30‑day average, a bounce back to the 50‑day moving average, or a press release confirming a new contract or legal resolution.
expect_next.timing_regime: The APEX score indicates tight compression, suggesting the price is near a bottom. If the company can clear its legal and liquidity hurdles, a mean‑reversion bounce is likely within the next 2–3 months.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company clears its legal hurdles and secures the loan facility, the price could recover to roughly 50% of the pre‑crash level – a 100% upside from the current trough. This aligns with the historical success of deep‑crash signals, which have delivered double‑digit returns when the bounce occurs.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the court denies relief or the loan terms become untenable, the company could hit a liquidation threshold, leaving investors with a near‑total loss. The worst‑case downside is a 100% drop from the current price.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 2× (double the current price). Potential downside: 1× (complete wipe‑out). The asymmetry favours a long position if the catalysts materialise.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when price breaks above the 50‑day moving average with a volume surge of at least 150% of the 30‑day average. Use a 2‑3% stop below that average to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash or a small position until you see either a positive court decision or a confirmed new contract announcement. Watch for a 20% jump in the 10‑day average volume as a signal to act.
playbook.if_passing: None identified. The company has not shown any imminent delisting or bankruptcy filings.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Deeply discounted, legal‑hazy but potentially stabilising; if the court and financing issues resolve, the upside is large relative to the residual risk.
